query:
prove this: That's problem 1. Problem 2 is that even if the SM topic were correct, the specialist sub-agents (investigator, rust-dev, tester, validator, security-reviewer) run in separate
  sessions and never call context_cycle — they rely entirely on content heuristics to get attributed. Those heuristics fire when agents mention the feature ID in tool payloads
  (feature: parameter on context_search, or feature_cycle: on context_store). Specialist agents don't do either.\
\
They were subagents, not a separate session.. I've been told before that subagents still have the same session_id, so correctly attributing them should correctly attribute the subagents.

entries.title: context_get fails at agent startup when querying entry IDs not yet stored in that session
context_cycle topic must be the canonical feature ID — free-form descriptions break retrospective attribution silently
ADR-003 col-024: Structured Log Event on Primary-Path Fallback Activation
col-025 Wave 3: SubagentStart goal-present branch placement in ContextSearch arm
New MCP tools must include agent_id in params struct -- checklist gap in col-022
